description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade. The handle appears to be made from dark-stained hardwood, showing a natural grain and ergonomic curvature for a comfortable grip. The blade is metallic, with a matte, weathered finish and a single sharp edge, while the opposite side is squared off, forming a poll for striking. The transition between the handle and blade is seamless, with the blade securely mounted onto the handle, likely using a traditional wedged method. The axe shows signs of use, with minor surface wear on both the handle and blade, but no visible decorative engravings or branding. This tool is typical for chopping wood or general utility tasks in urban or suburban environments such as garages, parks, or maintenance sheds.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade, designed for chopping or utility work. The handle is dark and ergonomic, while the blade is single-edged and robust.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat face of the metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the squared poll of the blade.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the blade and the end of the handle, showing the mounting joint.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, dark-stained hardwood handle with an ergonomic grip and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a squared poll and a broad, flat face; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
